2606.27405.title: Automated brain tumor detection in MRI images using CNN and ResNet architectures
2606.27405.paper_summary: Deep learning has shown significant potential in medical image analysis, particularly for disease detection using MRI scans. Accurate and early diagnosis of brain tumors remains challenging due to the complexity of brain structures and reliance on manual interpretation. This work presents an automated deep learning-based approach for brain tumor detection from MRI images using Convolutional Neural Networks and Residual Networks. Transfer learning is applied with two pretrained architectures, ResNet18 and ResNet50, to classify MRI scans into tumor and non-tumor categories. Experiments are conducted on a dataset of 3,929 brain MRI images, evaluating the impact of model depth and fine-tuning strategies. The results show that ResNet18 achieves a higher accuracy of 97% compared to 96% for ResNet50, demonstrating better generalization on limited medical data. The proposed framework enables fast, accurate, and cost-effective brain tumor detection, supporting early diagnosis and clinical decision-making.
2606.27405.paper_summary_zh: 深度學習在醫學影像分析中顯示出顯著的潛力，特別是在使用 MRI 掃描進行疾病檢測方面。由於腦部結構的複雜性和對手動解釋的依賴，準確且早期的腦腫瘤診斷仍然具有挑戰性。這項工作提出了一種基於深度學習的自動化方法，利用卷積神經網絡和殘差網絡從 MRI 圖像中檢測腦腫瘤。使用兩種預訓練架構 ResNet18 和 ResNet50 應用遷移學習，將 MRI 掃描分類為腫瘤和非腫瘤類別。對 3,929 張腦部 MRI 圖像的數據集進行了實驗，評估模型深度和微調策略的影響。結果顯示，ResNet18 的準確率達到 97%，而 ResNet50 為 96%，在有限的醫療數據上顯示出更好的泛化能力。所提出的框架實現了快速、準確且具成本效益的腦腫瘤檢測，支持早期診斷和臨床決策。
2606.27405.author: Annapurna V K et.al.
2606.27405.authors: Annapurna V K, Asha N, K Paramesha, Shabana Sultana, Kirankumar Humse
